summary_one_line: Input must contain a `<!N...` markup token inside the DTD so switch(NXT(2)) dispatches case 'N' to xmlParseNotationDecl.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Inside an external/internal DTD subset, the input must present a markup-declaration token whose third character after `<` is 'N' (i.e. `<!N...`, a NOTATION declaration), so that switch (NXT(2)) in xmlParseMarkupDecl selects case 'N'. The loser inputs only ever present `<!E`, `<!A`, `<!-` markup tokens (ELEMENT/ATTLIST/comment), never `<!N`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry a well-formed `<?xml version="1.0"?>` + `<!DOCTYPE` document and, at d=1, take the 'N' switch arm (case 'N': T=6 F=0) reaching the blocker line 6966, while losers stay on case 'E'/'A'. The value_profile_cmplog arm resolved 9/10 trials vs 0/10 for cmplog, and reached the branch 4 times vs 0 (avg hitcount winner=4 loser=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds exercise the same DOCTYPE scaffolding but at d=1 land on case 'E' (T=273 F=110) and case 'A' (T=107 F=276) and never on case 'N' (T=0 F=383); xmlParseElementDecl is hit 271 times for L and 0 for W. The loser explores ELEMENT/ATTLIST declarations exhaustively but never synthesizes the `<!N` notation token, so the case 'N' true side stays globally blocked for cmplog.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The only technique difference between value_profile_cmplog (winner) and cmplog (loser) is value_profile, i.e. the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient feedback that value_profile_cmplog adds over cmplog. Both fuzzers already share the same input-to-state comparison-solving mutator, so I2S cannot be the differentiator here. The CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs that get progressively closer (byte-by-byte) to satisfying the NXT(2)=='N' dispatch and the downstream 'NOTATION' keyword comparisons in xmlParseNotationDecl, giving partial-match credit that the pure edge-bucket signal of cmplog never sees, so vpc climbs toward the 'N' arm while cmplog stalls on E/A.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case 'N' arm of switch (NXT(2)) in xmlParseMarkupDecl, dispatching to xmlParseNotationDecl.
Taking the true side of the blocker calls xmlParseNotationDecl.
The winner takes the case 'N' arm while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L6966  T=6 F=0  T=0 F=383  case 'N':  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser exhaustively explores the case 'E' and case 'A' arms instead.
xmlParseElementDecl is invoked 271 times for the loser and never for the winner, showing the loser is stuck on ELEMENT declarations.
value_profile_cmplog resolved the branch in 9/10 trials and reached it 4 times on average; cmplog resolved 0/10 and reached it 0 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=4  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The single decisive pair differs by the value_profile technique only.
value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog, which is the only feedback difference between winner and loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: Both winner and loser already have I2SRandReplace, so I2S cannot be the differentiator.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient, not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory db_query.py lineage I2S-floor check does not apply. Both involved fuzzers (cmplog loser, value_profile_cmplog winner) already carry I2SRandReplace per the MECHANISM CONTEXT section, so a dash-row in the winner's lineage would not distinguish the pair — the sole technique delta is the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that value_profile_cmplog adds over cmplog (DECISIVE PAIRS: delta: value_profile). No lineage query was run because it cannot adjudicate a value_profile-delta pair.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only gate is switch(NXT(2))=='N' (a single 1-byte dispatch) where value_profile_cmplog shows no advantage over cmplog — i.e. cmplog resolves it as often as vpc — would refute that the CMP_MAP gradient is what drives reaching the 'N' arm.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the CMP_MAP gradient specifically aids the single-byte NXT(2)=='N' dispatch versus the multi-byte 'NOTATION' keyword comparisons downstream is not directly observable from the byte diff, which is dominated by shared DOCTYPE scaffolding rather than a concentrated N-token signal.
